human_verification.notes: Reviewed the food-service-and-restaurants food-safety package for boundary quality, authority-source quality, hold-release logic, guest-impact incident handling, payment and refund boundaries, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to restaurant food-safety review, hold-release control, incident and nonconformance handling, and evidence-backed escalation rather than final authority.

Authority sources cover FDA Food Code, FDA retail-food program guidance, CDC and CIFOR foodborne-incident handling, FDA menu-labeling guidance, CFPB payment-dispute guidance, and PCI DSS with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover contradictory evidence, release with open hold, repeat incidents, high-volume service triage, customer exception handling, refund and pricing boundary rejection, order and payment mismatch, policy conflicts, and tenant-specific restaurant overlays.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/food-safety-restaurants-specialist/ to satisfy the issue's canonical overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around medical advice, public-health authority, refund approvals, pricing overrides, fraud findings, and release-bypass requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated restaurant food-safety overlay where public-health, legal, pricing, refund, and fraud authority remain external.

The specialist is intentionally collaboration-oriented: it knows when to hand work to guest recovery, pricing, restaurant operations, loss prevention, QA leadership, medical teams, and public-health authorities.
Functionality coverage is complete and matches evaluation/results.json.
